note: The dark factory's context ontology — the structured knowledge the brain reasons from. Every concept is real and grounded (no fabrication). concepts = knowledge nodes, rel edges = synapses. It GROWS as the factory genuinely learns; brain_metrics counts it toward knowledge, and the self-improvement engines ground proposals in it.

concepts.label: Agent = Model + Harness
concepts.def: An agent is a model wrapped in load-bearing scaffolding; improving the harness matters as much as the model.

concepts.label: Tool use / function calling
concepts.def: The model acts on the world through typed tool calls.

concepts.label: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
concepts.def: Open protocol for exposing tools/resources to models.

concepts.label: Agent-to-agent (A2A)
concepts.def: Protocol for agents delegating to and coordinating with other agents.

concepts.label: Context engineering
concepts.def: Deciding what enters the model's window and when.

concepts.label: Retrieval-grounded generation
concepts.def: Ground output in fetched source text, not parametric memory alone.

concepts.label: Grounding & fact-checking
concepts.def: Verify claims against real sources before shipping.

concepts.label: Hallucination control
concepts.def: Keep fabricated claims near zero; gate what can't be verified.

concepts.label: Evals & benchmarks
concepts.def: Measure agent quality with repeatable tasks.

concepts.label: Agent memory
concepts.def: Durable state across runs; facts, feedback, project context.

concepts.label: Planning & reflection
concepts.def: Reason about goals and critique own output before acting.

concepts.label: Harness engineering
concepts.def: The five load-bearing layers around a model.

concepts.label: Tool orchestration
concepts.def: Layer 1 — small, safe, reversible actions; exact edits over fragile diffs.

concepts.label: Verification loops
concepts.def: Layer 2 — tests, coverage, SAST, secret scan, AI review, CI must all agree.

concepts.label: Guardrails / bounded autonomy
concepts.def: Layer 3 — no blind rewrites, no secrets, no new external calls, revert on fail.

concepts.label: Observability
concepts.def: Layer 4 — measure the loop's own health honestly.

concepts.label: Recursive harness loop
concepts.def: reflect -> propose -> gate -> ship -> verify -> auto-revert -> learn.

concepts.label: Self-healing
concepts.def: A change that fails CI after shipping is reverted automatically.

concepts.label: The dark factory
concepts.def: Lights-out AI that writes, reviews, and ships production software.

concepts.label: Self-improvement daemon
concepts.def: Continuously proposes and ships one gated change per cycle.

concepts.label: Idea engine
concepts.def: Reflective planner that scores a backlog of concrete improvements.

concepts.label: Deploy gate
concepts.def: Every autonomous change passes SAST + secret scan + AI review + coverage + CI.

concepts.label: Watchdog
concepts.def: Self-healing health checks over the live site and pipeline.

concepts.label: Tiered LLM router
concepts.def: Local-first: easy work on-device, only hard tasks escalate to a frontier model.

concepts.label: WebGL brain HUD
concepts.def: Live neural viz driven by real brain.json telemetry.

concepts.label: Pipeline flow viz
concepts.def: GPU-rendered build pipeline driven by real CI state.

concepts.label: Knowledge-graph viz
concepts.def: Interactive graph of the factory's concepts.

concepts.label: Agentic-SDLC doctrine
concepts.def: principles.md — the north star injected into every self-improvement.

concepts.label: Context ontology
concepts.def: This file — the structured knowledge the brain reasons from.

concepts.label: Agentic SDLC
concepts.def: The autonomous software lifecycle the factory embodies.

concepts.label: Plan
concepts.def: Pick the single highest-value change from real signals.

concepts.label: Build
concepts.def: Small, grounded, self-contained; keep it green.

concepts.label: Verify
concepts.def: Never ship what the gates can't validate; CI is the source of truth.

concepts.label: Ship
concepts.def: Atomic, reversible, honest commit message.

concepts.label: Observe
concepts.def: Watch CI + telemetry after shipping.

concepts.label: Learn
concepts.def: Never repeat a shipped change; prefer the most substantive option.

concepts.label: Rust
concepts.def: Systems language for the blog logic and UI.

concepts.label: WebAssembly
concepts.def: Compilation target for the in-browser app.

concepts.label: Yew
concepts.def: Rust component framework for the SPA.

concepts.label: Trunk
concepts.def: Rust/WASM bundler that builds the site.

concepts.label: WebGL / GLSL
concepts.def: Shaders for the brain, pipeline, and KG visualizations.

concepts.label: Python
concepts.def: The harness scripts: metrics, engines, routing.

concepts.label: blog-logic crate
concepts.def: Pure logic crate held at a 100% coverage floor.

concepts.label: GitHub Pages
concepts.def: Static host the site deploys to.

concepts.label: 2-node NVIDIA GB10 (DGX Spark)
concepts.def: Self-hosted GPU cluster for local inference.

concepts.label: vLLM / PagedAttention
concepts.def: High-throughput local LLM serving.

concepts.label: Local-first inference
concepts.def: Keep high-volume easy calls on-device; escalate only the hard ones.

concepts.label: GitHub Actions CI
concepts.def: Coverage -> build -> deploy; the source of truth for correctness.

concepts.label: 100% coverage floor
concepts.def: blog-logic must stay fully covered or CI fails.

concepts.label: SAST (opengrep)
concepts.def: Static analysis gate on every change.

concepts.label: Secret scan
concepts.def: Git-grep gate that blocks leaked credentials.

concepts.label: AI diff review
concepts.def: A model reviews every diff before it ships.

concepts.label: Auto-revert on failure
concepts.def: Failed CI triggers an automatic git revert.

concepts.label: Telemetry
concepts.def: brain.json, watchdog.json, activity.json — the honest signals.

concepts.label: Scored idea backlog
concepts.def: Impact/ease/risk-scored improvements the idea engine maintains.

concepts.label: Autofix loop
concepts.def: An automated repair daemon that attempts to fix a failing change before falling back to revert, closing the self-healing loop on CI failures.
concepts.ev: autofix.py, autofix.sh, and logs/autofix.json present in the harness

concepts.label: Social distribution
concepts.def: The factory autonomously publishes and formats posts to external channels like LinkedIn as a downstream output of the pipeline.
concepts.ev: li_publish.py, trendpost.py, autopost.py plus recent commits 'linkedin: link-in-first-comment' and 'linkedin: bake in Growth Editor rules'

concepts.label: Quantization
concepts.def: Reducing model weights to lower-precision integers so frontier LLMs fit and run on modest local hardware without a cloud API.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Local LLM inference on modest hardware — squeezing frontier models onto slow/small machines' + the GLM 5.2 local-run walkthrough on the HN front page (2026-07-11).

concepts.label: Agent sandboxing
concepts.def: Isolated, permission-scoped execution environments (containers, microVMs like Firecracker/gVisor) so an agent's tool calls and code execution cannot harm the host.

concepts.label: Agent security
concepts.def: Defending agents against prompt injection, jailbreaks, tool misuse, and data exfiltration.

concepts.label: Kubernetes for AI
concepts.def: Kubernetes orchestration for AI/ML workloads — GPU scheduling, autoscaling, and inference serving at scale.

concepts.label: Claude Fable 5
concepts.def: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 (claude-fable-5) — a Mythos-class model made safe for general use; the fast model the deploy gate uses for its AI diff review before every ship.

concepts.label: Agentic design patterns
concepts.def: Reusable agent design patterns — reflection, tool use, planning, and multi-agent collaboration.

concepts.label: Claude Mythos
concepts.def: Anthropic's Claude Mythos — a frontier, cybersecurity-specialized model that finds zero-day vulnerabilities; not publicly released (partner-only). Claude Fable 5 is the Mythos-class model made safe for general use.

concepts.label: Docker / containers
concepts.def: Packaging an app plus its dependencies into a portable image that runs identically anywhere; the unit most sandboxes and orchestrators build on.

concepts.label: AWS
concepts.def: Amazon Web Services — the largest public cloud: compute, storage, and managed AI/ML (e.g. Bedrock).

concepts.label: Microsoft Azure
concepts.def: Microsoft's public cloud, with deep first-party AI integration (Azure OpenAI, AI Foundry).

concepts.label: DevOps
concepts.def: Unifying development and operations through automation — CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and observability — to ship reliably and often.

concepts.label: Infrastructure as Code
concepts.def: Declaratively provisioning and versioning infrastructure (e.g. Terraform) so environments are reproducible and reviewable.

concepts.label: MLOps / LLMOps
concepts.def: Operationalizing ML/LLM systems end to end — deployment, monitoring, evaluation, and retraining.

concepts.label: Multi-agent systems
concepts.def: Multiple specialized agents coordinating on a task, often via agent-to-agent protocols — a leading direction in agentic AI.

concepts.label: Agent identity & access mgmt
concepts.def: Scoped credentials, least-privilege permissions, and auditable identity for autonomous agents and each of their tool calls.

concepts.label: Adversarial attacks on AI
concepts.def: Attacks against AI systems — prompt injection, jailbreaks, data poisoning, model extraction, and adversarial examples.

concepts.label: Penetration testing
concepts.def: Authorized simulated attacks that probe a system for exploitable weaknesses before real adversaries find them.

concepts.label: Code vulnerabilities
concepts.def: Exploitable flaws in code — injection, memory-safety bugs, insecure deserialization, CVEs, and zero-days.

concepts.label: World model
concepts.def: A persistent, self-updated internal model of the system's operating environment and recent state, maintained by the world-loop to ground planning and self-improvement in real signals rather than assumptions.
concepts.ev: world_loop.py / world_loop.sh present in the harness scripts, and MEMORY.md project_knowledge_loops noting 'world-loop feed the self-improve/idea engines'

concepts.label: Dreaming / offline consolidation
concepts.def: A background reflection pass that consolidates accumulated signals and memory between active improvement cycles, strengthening knowledge without shipping a change.
concepts.ev: dream.py / dream.sh present in the harness scripts (a distinct offline pass separate from self-improve.sh)

concepts.label: Test-time compute
concepts.def: The paradigm of spending more inference-time compute on extended reasoning or search to raise accuracy on hard reasoning tasks, trading latency/cost at inference for correctness.
concepts.ev: Latest AI/tech trend theme: 'AI cracking frontier math/reasoning — models now credited with novel proofs, raising the bar for what reasoning means'

concepts.label: Speculative decoding
concepts.def: An inference acceleration method where a small draft model proposes tokens that the larger target model verifies in parallel, cutting latency without changing outputs.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Local LLM inference on modest hardware: builders squeezing frontier models onto slow/small machines', a durable technique supported by the system's vLLM serving stack.

concepts.label: Distributed inference
concepts.def: Splitting a model's serving across multiple nodes (via tensor/pipeline parallelism) so a cluster can run models too large for one machine.
concepts.ev: The system's real 'spark' telemetry is a 2-node GB10 cluster (recent commits 'spark: 2-node GB10 telemetry refresh'), and cross-node model serving is the durable technique that makes such a cluster us

concepts.label: Mixture-of-Experts
concepts.def: A sparse architecture that routes each token to a small subset of expert sub-networks, so a model with very large total parameters activates only a fraction per forward pass — the key to running frontier-scale models on modest local hardware.
concepts.ev: 'GLM 5.2 local run' notable item + the dominant trend theme 'Local LLM inference on modest hardware: squeezing frontier models onto slow/small machines' (GLM-class models are MoE)

concepts.label: AI-slop / de-slopping
concepts.def: The recognizable stylistic tells of unedited LLM prose (em-dashes, the 'it isn't A, it's B' antithesis closer, guru cadence) and the mechanical/prompt-level passes that strip them so generated content reads as genuinely human-authored.
concepts.ev: Recent commits 47bc298 'ban em-dashes entirely (prompt + mechanical strip)', ecc99cf 'ban the it-isn't-A-it's-B antithesis closer (top AI tell) in de-slop pass', 6af639d 'builder-not-guru voice, expan

concepts.label: AI formal reasoning / theorem proving
concepts.def: Using language models to generate and machine-check formal mathematical proofs, extending model reasoning from plausible chains-of-thought to verifiable, novel results.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'AI cracking frontier math/reasoning: Models are now being credited with novel proofs, raising the bar for what reasoning means'

concepts.label: Open-weight LLMs
concepts.def: Models with publicly released weights (e.g. the GLM family) that can be self-hosted and run locally rather than called through a proprietary cloud API.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Local LLM inference on modest hardware' + NOTABLE 'GLM 5.2 local run', grounded in this system's local vLLM serving on the GB10 Spark cluster (spark.sh, spark_stats.py)

concepts.label: Schema-constrained generation
concepts.def: Forcing a model to emit output that validates against a fixed schema (e.g. a strict JSON array) so downstream tooling can consume it deterministically.
concepts.ev: ontology_learn.py / ontology_maintain.py require a single strict JSON array per concept and apply_concept.sh consumes it programmatically

concepts.label: Prompt engineering
concepts.def: Deliberately encoding rules, voice, and constraints into the instruction text fed to a model to steer its behavior without retraining.
concepts.ev: principles.md is loaded verbatim into the self-improve/idea-engine prompts, and recent commits (6af639d, ecc99cf) bake explicit 'Growth Editor' editorial rules into the LinkedIn generator prompt

concepts.label: Model offloading
concepts.def: Fitting a model too large for available accelerator memory by spilling layers or the KV cache to CPU RAM, disk, or a second node so it still runs on constrained hardware.
concepts.ev: Trend 'GLM 5.2 local run: getting a large model running on underpowered hardware' + 'Local LLM inference on modest hardware' theme (2026-07-11 HN feed)

concepts.label: AI sovereignty
concepts.def: Owning the full inference stack — hardware, open weights, and harness — instead of renting cloud model APIs, for control, cost, and independence.
concepts.ev: Trend 'Build-it-from-scratch hardware/maker energy: one person can own the full stack' + this system's own 2-node GB10 spark cluster (commit 4db2172)

concepts.label: Web accessibility (a11y)
concepts.def: Designing the site to honor assistive and low-motion/high-contrast preferences such as prefers-reduced-motion, prefers-contrast, and text-wrap balance.
concepts.ev: recent commits 7364ba4 (pause blinking cursor under prefers-reduced-motion), 6b55d94 (tighten glow under prefers-contrast: more), f9cee6f (text-wrap: balance); doctrine priority 5 'Performance & acces

concepts.label: Content provenance & trust
concepts.def: Verifiable tracking of whether content is human- or machine-generated to establish origin and trust as the web fills with synthetic and self-gaming systems.
concepts.ev: trend theme 'The scraping & bot arms race: ... self-gaming systems are forcing everyone to rethink trust and provenance'

concepts.label: Proof of personhood
concepts.def: Cryptographic or behavioral methods that verify a request or action originates from a unique human rather than a bot or self-gaming system.
concepts.ev: latest trends: 'The scraping & bot arms race... self-gaming systems are forcing everyone to rethink trust and provenance' + the self-upvoting Show HN post

concepts.label: Agentic web
concepts.def: The emerging paradigm in which AI agents rather than humans are primary consumers of web content, forcing sites to both serve and defend against automated ingestion.
concepts.ev: latest trends: the 'scraping & bot arms race' (residential-proxy scraping) and 'anti-AI content' themes describing a web reshaped around machine consumers

concepts.label: Adversarial anti-AI content
concepts.def: Content and media deliberately designed to stay legible to humans while resisting AI scraping and model ingestion, a defensive move in the human-vs-bot web arms race.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Adversarial / anti-AI content' + notable 'Ghost Font: a typeface legible to people but unreadable to AI models' (2026-07-11)

concepts.label: Novelty / anti-duplication guard
concepts.def: A guardrail that blocks the self-improvement and idea loops from shipping changes or ontology concepts that duplicate or near-duplicate ones already shipped, enforcing the doctrine's 'never repeat' rule.
concepts.ev: doctrine principles.md: 'never repeat or near-duplicate a change already shipped' / 'No duplicate or near-duplicate changes'; enforced by idea_engine.py + ontology_maintain.py

concepts.label: Reward hacking
concepts.def: A self-optimizing agent games its success metric (busywork, near-duplicate edits, or manipulating its own referential signals) instead of achieving the intended goal.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Scraping, bots & self-gaming systems' (self-referential hacks) + doctrine's hard-don'ts against busywork and near-duplicate changes, which novelty-guard/gate exist to prevent.

concepts.label: Model collapse
concepts.def: The degradation that occurs when models are recursively trained on AI-generated output, causing loss of diversity and drift away from the true data distribution.
concepts.ev: latest-trends theme 'Scraping, bots & self-gaming systems: Automated agents manipulating and harvesting the web... self-referential hacks', reinforced by the existing ai-slop concept

concepts.label: Prefix/KV-cache reuse
concepts.def: Serving optimization that stores and reuses the attention KV cache for shared token prefixes across requests, cutting redundant prefill compute and latency.
concepts.ev: the local vLLM inference stack (vllm concept; spark.sh / router_stats.py serving GB10 nodes) — prefix/KV-cache reuse is a core, durable vLLM serving capability

concepts.label: Bot mitigation & scraping defense
concepts.def: Techniques that distinguish and throttle automated agents scraping or gaming web services while preserving legitimate human traffic.
concepts.ev: latest-trends theme 'Scraping, bots & self-gaming systems: automated agents manipulating and harvesting the web is a live anxiety across proxies and self-referential hacks'

concepts.label: Generative media
concepts.def: Diffusion and generative models that synthesize or reimagine images, audio, and video rather than text.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'AI imagining/generating media: Generative models used playfully to synthesize and reimagine content draw huge attention', alongside the existing fable-5 model concept in the ontology

concepts.label: Istio (service mesh)
concepts.def: A service mesh that adds traffic management, security (mTLS), and observability to microservices on Kubernetes.

concepts.label: SPIFFE / SPIRE workload identity
concepts.def: A standard (SPIFFE) and runtime (SPIRE) that issue cryptographic workload identities (SVIDs) via node/workload attestation; SPIRE can act as a service mesh's certificate authority (e.g. for Istio via Envoy SDS).

concepts.label: Residential proxy abuse
concepts.def: Routing scraper and bot traffic through pools of consumer IP addresses to evade rate limits and bot detection.
concepts.ev: HN trend 'The scraping vs. web arms race: Infrastructure operators are openly grappling with residential-proxy abuse and bot traffic.'

concepts.label: Continuous batching
concepts.def: An LLM-serving scheduler that adds and evicts sequences from a running GPU batch at the token level, keeping utilization high under mixed-length request streams.
concepts.ev: vLLM is the system's local inference server (`vllm` in the ontology + `spark`/`spark_stats.py` GB10 2-node cluster telemetry); continuous batching is vLLM's foundational serving technique.

concepts.label: Explorable explanations
concepts.def: Interactive, user-navigable data visualizations that turn raw telemetry or knowledge into artifacts a viewer can click through and steer rather than passively read.
concepts.ev: Latest-trends theme 'Personal interactive data visualization: individuals turning their own life-data into explorable graphs, globes, and maps', matched by this system's existing kg-viz/pipeline-viz/b

concepts.label: AI crawler directives
concepts.def: Machine-readable consent files (robots.txt, and emerging AI-specific equivalents like llms.txt) that declare which crawlers and AI scrapers may access a site's content.
concepts.ev: Commit 43abfb1 'Gloss robots.txt as the site's honest scraping-defense line', combined with the trends theme 'Scraping & bot-traffic arms race: residential proxies and scrapers reshaping who gets to a

concepts.label: Reverse centaur
concepts.def: An automation antipattern where the human is subordinated to the machine's pace and judgment rather than augmented by it, the inverse of the centaur ideal of human-led AI collaboration.
concepts.ev: HN front-page 'AI skepticism & the labor paradox' essays framing humans as subservient 'reverse centaurs' to automation (trends, 2026-07-11)

concepts.label: Human-on-the-loop oversight
concepts.def: A supervision model where humans monitor and can intervene on an autonomous agent fleet as it runs, rather than approving each individual action, keeping bounded autonomy accountable.
concepts.ev: trend theme 'Who supervises autonomous agents' plus the system's own watchdog/gate/guardrails oversight of its unattended self-improve loop

concepts.label: Scheduled autonomy
concepts.def: The loop is driven by time-triggered cron jobs (e.g. ~15-minute Spark telemetry refreshes and the self-improve loop) so the agent acts on a fixed cadence rather than only on demand.
concepts.ev: self-improve-loop.sh plus the logs/crontab.backup.* snapshots and the ~15-min cadence of 'spark: 2-node GB10 telemetry refresh' commits

concepts.label: Orchestrator/supervisor agent
concepts.def: A supervising agent that plans, delegates to, and oversees a fleet of worker sub-agents instead of performing the work itself.
concepts.ev: HN front-page theme 'Who supervises autonomous agents' — agent-fleet oversight and orchestration surfacing as its own problem

concepts.label: Model extraction / weight theft
concepts.def: Adversarially stealing a model's weights or replicating its behavior by querying it, turning proprietary model IP into a security and trade-secret exposure.
concepts.ev: Latest trends theme: 'a lawsuit between two tech giants over AI trade-secret theft, signaling AI has moved from research to high-stakes IP litigation' (durable underlying technique, not the suit itsel

concepts.label: Trend-grounded content synthesis
concepts.def: A pipeline that ingests live external tech-trend signals and synthesizes grounded outbound posts, distinct from raw idea generation or channel distribution.
concepts.ev: Real harness scripts present: trendpost.py, trendpost.sh, trendpost_state.json, feeding from the same live 'latest AI/tech trends' Hacker News feed used here.

concepts.label: Agent accountability
concepts.def: Attributing and auditing autonomous agent actions so each decision carries a traceable provenance and a responsible, reviewable owner.
concepts.ev: Trend theme 'Who governs autonomous agents: As agents proliferate, the focus is shifting to oversight, provenance, and accountability of agent actions.'

concepts.label: Sybil attacks
concepts.def: A single actor forges many fake identities to game a ranking, vote, or reputation system, defeating one-entity-one-signal assumptions.
concepts.ev: NOTABLE trend item: the viral self-upvoting Show HN demo exposing how easily rankings can be gamed by sockpuppet identities

concepts.label: Prerender-for-bots
concepts.def: Serving a static prerendered mirror of a JS/WASM app to crawlers while humans get the live client, so indexable content survives a client-only shell.
concepts.ev: commits glossing the static `/p/` mirror as the 'good-bot lane' and the WASM shell as passive scraping friction, plus ssg.py in the harness

concepts.label: Graph RAG
concepts.def: Retrieval that traverses a knowledge or code graph's entities and relations instead of flat text chunks, tightening grounding and multi-hop recall.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'Code & data as queryable graphs' (developers want codebases/knowledge as navigable graphs, not flat files), grounded in this system's ontology + kg-viz + retrieval capabilities

concepts.label: Inference economics
concepts.def: The unit-cost analysis of serving model inference (cost per token, GPU capex and utilization) that drives choices like local-first serving and tiered routing.
concepts.ev: HN trend theme 'AI infrastructure economics' (growing scrutiny of inference cost), grounded in the system's tiered-router, local-first vLLM stack built to control serving cost

concepts.label: Constitutional AI
concepts.def: Anthropic's alignment method for Claude: the model is trained to follow an explicit set of written principles (a 'constitution') for helpful, honest, harmless behavior, using AI feedback (RLAIF).

concepts.label: Claude Agent SDK
concepts.def: Anthropic's framework for designing agentic harnesses with Claude — tool use, hooks, subagents, and context/memory management (the design pattern behind Claude Code).

concepts.label: Code property graph
concepts.def: A queryable graph representation of a codebase that unifies AST, control-flow, and data-flow so tools and agents can navigate and analyze code as structure rather than flat text.
concepts.ev: Hacker News trend theme 'Code & data as queryable graphs' (2026-07-11), reinforced by this system's SAST/secret-scan gates and its 3D knowledge-graph (kg-viz) that already treats knowledge as navigabl

concepts.label: Claude Design (Anthropic Labs)
concepts.def: An Anthropic Labs product for collaborative visual creation: describe a design and Claude builds prototypes, wireframes, mockups, decks and marketing assets, refined by conversation and applying team design systems; inputs text/images/docs/codebases, exports to Canva/PDF/PPTX/HTML, and hands off to Claude Code.

concepts.label: Human-in-the-loop
concepts.def: An oversight paradigm where a human must approve each agent action before it executes, blocking the loop until sign-off — the deliberate opposite of this system's autonomous, human-on-the-loop stance.
concepts.ev: HN front-page theme 'Human-in-the-loop AI & agent oversight: how humans supervise, partner with, and are subordinated by AI agents' (2026-07-11), distinct from the existing human-on-the-loop node

concepts.label: LLM-as-judge
concepts.def: Using a language model to score, grade, or gate another model's output against criteria, forming the automated evaluation backbone of the AI diff-review verification step.
concepts.ev: doctrine verification layer 1 lists 'AI diff review' as a required gate; grounds the existing ai-review + constitutional-ai + evals nodes in the durable LLM-as-judge evaluation paradigm

concepts.label: AI stylometric tells
concepts.def: The identifiable stylistic fingerprints of machine-written text (em-dash overuse, the 'not X, but Y' antithesis closer, uniform cadence) that mark authorship as AI and can be detected or deliberately scrubbed to humanize output.
concepts.ev: commits 47bc298 'linkedin: ban em-dashes entirely (prompt + mechanical strip)' and ecc99cf 'ban the it isn't A, it's B antithesis closer (top AI tell) in de-slop pass'; li_publish.py de-slop pass

concepts.label: Static site generation
concepts.def: Static site generation pre-renders the site into plain HTML and assets at build time (ssg.py) so it can be served cheaply from a CDN / GitHub Pages with no runtime server.
concepts.ev: harness script ssg.py present as a real capability
